content: We can reframe autonomous AI agents as active effect-producing entities that use a large language model integrated with a unified knowledge graph as their inherent structural 'power exertion backbone', where the unified graph encodes stable causal relationships between entities that the agent draws on to generate targeted real-world effects aligned with its definition as an acting, effect-producing system. This framework turns the unified graph from a static knowledge storage structure into a causal roadmap that empowers agents to move beyond pattern-matching text generation to deliberate, cause-effect oriented action.
source_topics: Large language model integration with unified graph
trigger_topic: Large language model integration with unified graph
